free_text: Study of 194Ir via thermal neutron capture
and (d, p) reactions
Gas-filled light-ion detector
High resolution Q3D spectrograph
The target consisted of 193Ir enriched to 98%. It had
an area of 1 x 4 mm2 and its thickness was 35 mu-g/cm2
on a 5 mu-g/cm2 thick carbon backing.
Gas-filled light-ion detector in the
focal plane of the high resolution Q3D spectrograph.
After the entrance foil, an incident particle passes
a multiwire proportional chamber, consisting of two
anode wires between a cathode plane foil and a cathode
strip foil.
The elastic-scattering line was observed by a
solid-state monitor detector.
Intensities of the lines in the position sensitive
detector were normalized using data from the monitor
counter.
Table 5 of Fizika B7(1998)15
SD
On. REFERENCE: 2 refs moved to REL-REF
Weighted mean values for level energies
and their errors were computed from the data at all
four angles.
Statistical error
